name: Cecil Bridgewater
abstract: Cecil Bridgewater (born October 10, 1942) is an American jazz trumpeter and composer.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: The In Crowd

works.t: Sometimes I Feel Like a Motherless Child
Were You There (When They Crucified My Lord)
Joshua Fit de Battle of Jericho

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Max Roach Quartet/ McCoy Tyner Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember, um, Cecil Bridgewater, who's now playing with, uh, Max Roach, uh, coming in one time... And that whole big band came down one time for a festival or some, for some performances. And we had Cecil and a lot of the players, uh, that would, that would pretty much our peers then... Whenever w

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee narrates an anecdote involving Cecil Bridgewater and Jim Anderson regarding a big band recording, but does not describe a direct personal or professional relationship between himself and Cecil Bridgewater.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: We organized this festival and I brought over Odetta, Max Roach’s without Max: Cecil Bridgewater, Billy Harper, Junior Booth, my son Azzedine played percussion.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: I did some things for the group. I remember one of the tunes I did – I don’t remember other things – only one I remember was I Remember Clifford, I think.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I had some dynamite folks on here, like Elvin Jones and Cecil Bridgewater...
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
